assertion.text: Erdős Problem #475 [status: decidable; formalized: no]. Let $p$ be a prime. Given any finite set $A\subseteq \mathbb{F}_p\backslash \{0\}$, is there always a rearrangement $A=\{a_1,\ldots,a_t\}$ such that all partial sums $\sum_{1\leq k\leq m}a_{k}$ are distinct, for all $1\leq m\leq t$? Current best: This has been proved for $t\leq 12$ (see Costa and Pellegrini \cite{CoPe20} and the references therein) and for $p-3\leq t\leq p-1$ (see Hicks, Ollis, and Schmitt \cite{HOS19} and the references therein). Prize: no. Tags: additive combinatorics, number theory.
